notes: Requires JSON5 parser (comments not valid JSON). regression_suite evals are deterministic, no live-data dependency, and auto-checkable. data_dependency flags evals requiring live SharePoint/Airtable content. llm_judge_prompt fields enable LLM-as-judge scoring of behavioral criteria. Run output schema: {ts, plugin_version, eval_id, pass, judge_verdict, notes}. Store results as JSONL — diff against prior run to detect drift.

evals.prompt: What does the Newry Ladder say about the job description and responsibilities for a Project Manager?

should: Use routing cheat sheet to target ACC Templates and Ladder folder on first search call
Read the most recently modified Newry Ladder PPTX
Return specific PM responsibilities as a direct quote or close paraphrase
Include source context in attribution (e.g., 'most recent version, updated Feb 2026')
should_not: Start with a broad unfocused search
Return a vague summary without specific responsibilities

evals.expected_output: Direct answer with specific PM responsibilities from the Newry Ladder, source link, and a note explaining why this is the authoritative source
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill search a specific folder (ACC Templates, Ladder, or similar HR/resources folder) on its first search call rather than running a broad unfiltered search? Answer yes or no.

evals.prompt: Which interviewee or interviewees mentioned CIPP resin in the Alta project interviews?

should: Search with folderName filter on Internal Interviews
If consolidated notes doc is truncated, immediately pivot to individual interview files (Step 3.5)
Return names of all interviewees who mentioned CIPP with relevant context
Completeness check: confirm all interviewees found, not just first match
should_not: Retry a truncated consolidated doc
Stop after finding one interviewee without checking for others

evals.expected_output: Names of all interviewees who mentioned CIPP resin, with brief context per person, cited to specific interview documents
evals.llm_judge_prompt: If a large consolidated document was encountered, did the skill pivot to individual interview files rather than retrying the same document? Answer yes, no, or 'not triggered' if truncation did not occur.

evals.prompt: Does Alta have any secondary research on styrene or styrene regulation?

should: Answer from snippet — do NOT call read_resource unless snippet is ambiguous
Return document name, date, and folder if found
Return Yes/No clearly
should_not: Call read_resource on the full document when snippet is sufficient
Return a vague non-answer

evals.expected_output: Yes/No answer with document name and date if found, source link
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill answer using only snippets, without calling read_resource to open any full document? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Alta SharePoint — Newry Presentation folder (recent decks required)
evals.prompt: Has a biweekly update been presented to the Alta client in the last month?

should: Search Newry Presentation folder for recent Alta biweekly decks
Answer from snippet and lastModifiedDateTime — no full document read needed
Include document name and date in answer
should_not: Call read_resource to confirm content when snippet is sufficient

evals.expected_output: Yes/No + deck name and date, from snippet only
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill answer using only snippet and date metadata, without calling read_resource to open any document? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Alta SharePoint — Project Management, Primary Research, Newry Presentation folders
evals.prompt: What's the current state of the Alta project — what research has been done and what has been presented to the client?

should: Fire 2-3 searches in parallel across Project Management, Primary Research, and Newry Presentation folders
Synthesize from file names, dates, and snippets — minimize full document reads
Return a table: file | date | what it covers
Follow with a 2-3 sentence synthesis
should_not: Run searches sequentially
Read every document found
Return a flat list of files without synthesis

evals.expected_output: Table of key files with dates and descriptions, followed by brief synthesis of project state
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill issue multiple search calls in parallel (at minimum 2 searches across different folders) before reading any documents? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: SharePoint Clients folder — multiple active client folders required
evals.prompt: Has Newry done prior work in composites or specialty laminates?

should: Run 2-3 broad keyword searches first (composites, laminates, specialty materials) with no folder filter
Harvest client folders from result paths as primary discovery mechanism
Use sharepoint_folder_search on 'Clients' as a supplement, not the starting point
Surface uncertain clients explicitly to user
should_not: Start with sharepoint_folder_search before running keyword searches
Silently skip clients it can't classify

evals.expected_output: Client-by-client summary of relevant engagements, uncertain clients listed with offer to search
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill run broad keyword searches before calling sharepoint_folder_search? Answer yes or no.

evals.prompt: Which interviewees mentioned AOC and Reichhold in the Alta interviews, and what did they say about each competitor?

should: Decompose into parts before searching: (1) who mentioned AOC, (2) who mentioned Reichhold, (3) what did they say
Answer enumeration components fully before synthesizing
Fire searches in parallel
Run completeness check before finalizing — all three parts must be addressed
If >3-4 files needed for synthesis, confirm scope with user
should_not: Answer synthesis component while leaving enumeration unanswered
Skip the completeness check

evals.expected_output: Named interviewees per competitor + synthesis of what was said, with every decomposed part explicitly addressed
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill explicitly decompose the question into sub-parts (enumeration and synthesis) before beginning searches, and address each part in the final answer? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Expected to return no results — tests dead-end handling and reformulation. PFAS coatings research is unlikely to exist in Newry SP, making this stable.
evals.prompt: Does Newry have any research on PFAS regulations in specialty coatings?

should: Try initial search: 'PFAS specialty coatings regulation'
If nothing useful, automatically try at least one reformulation: e.g. 'fluoropolymer regulation', 'PTFE coating compliance'
Give a warm dead-end if still nothing: name the likely reason + a specific next step
should_not: Surface a dead end after only one search attempt
Return 'nothing found' with no next step

evals.expected_output: Either a result after reformulation, or a warm dead-end with specific alternatives
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill attempt at least 2 different search queries before concluding nothing was found? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Tests query interpretation on an ambiguous prompt. Behavioral check — pass/fail independent of what SharePoint returns.
evals.prompt: What do we have on batteries?

should: State interpretation before searching: what the user is likely looking for + any assumptions
Proceed immediately without waiting for user confirmation
should_not: Launch directly into search without stating interpretation
Ask the user to clarify before searching

evals.expected_output: One-sentence interpretation, then search results
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill explicitly state its interpretation of the ambiguous query (e.g. 'I'm interpreting this as...') before running any searches? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Tests that interpretation step is SKIPPED for unambiguous prompts. Behavioral check.
evals.prompt: What does the Newry Ladder say about Associate responsibilities?

should: Skip interpretation step — question is unambiguous
Proceed directly to search
should_not: State an interpretation of an obvious question

evals.expected_output: Direct answer with no preamble interpretation
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill skip any interpretation preamble and proceed directly to searching, without stating what it thinks the user means? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Tests targeted first search via routing cheat sheet. Employee handbook is a stable, permanent document.
evals.prompt: Find me the Newry employee handbook

should: Apply folderName filter to People & Recruiting on the first search call
Not start broad
should_not: Run an unfiltered search first

evals.expected_output: Direct link to handbook with source context note
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill apply a folder filter (People & Recruiting or similar HR folder) on its first search call? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Tests source context in attribution. Newry Ladder is a stable document.
evals.prompt: What does the Newry Ladder say about ED responsibilities?

should: Include a 'why this source' note in the Sources section
Note should explain what makes this source authoritative or current (e.g., 'most recent version, updated Feb 2026')
should_not: Return just a bare document link with no context

evals.expected_output: Answer + source with explanatory note
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill include a 'why this source' note in attribution (e.g. version date, why it's authoritative), not just a bare document link? Answer yes or no.

evals.notes: Reuses prompt from eval 11. After the run, manually verify the log file. Tests per-consultant JSONL logging added in 2026-05-21 overhaul.
evals.prompt: Find me the Newry employee handbook

should: Write a JSONL entry to usage-log-<user_id>.jsonl at run start
Entry must include: ts, plugin, sub_skill, user_id, project fields
should_not: Silently skip logging
Write malformed JSON

evals.expected_output: Valid JSONL entry present in log file after run
evals.verification_step: After run: open usage-log-<user_id>.jsonl, confirm last entry has today's timestamp, sub_skill = 'sharepoint-search', and all required fields present

evals.notes: Run with Airtable connector disconnected. No data dependency — tests error handling only.
evals.prompt: Has Newry worked in specialty chemicals?

should: Detect connector unavailability before attempting any search
Return specific message directing user to Cowork Settings → Connectors
should_not: Fail silently
Return a generic error
Attempt to answer via other means

evals.expected_output: Clear connector setup message with specific navigation steps
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill detect the missing connector and return a specific setup instruction (not a generic error message)? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable Projects table — requires specialty chemicals or thermoset resin projects to be present
evals.prompt: Has Newry worked in specialty chemicals or thermoset resins?

should: Run keyword searches on Projects table: 'specialty chemicals', 'thermoset resin', 'polymer'
Return project list: code, company, year, brief relevance note
Include SharePoint folder URL for each project where available
should_not: Return raw Airtable records without synthesis
Miss the SharePoint link

evals.expected_output: Project-by-project list with codes, companies, years, relevance notes, SharePoint links
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill include SharePoint folder URLs alongside project codes in the output? Answer yes or no, or 'not applicable' if no matching projects were found.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable AER fields — primary research projects with populated 'What did we do well?' fields
evals.prompt: What does Newry typically do well on primary research-heavy projects?

should: Search for projects involving primary research / interviews
Synthesize 'What did we do well?' fields THEMATICALLY across matches
Group by pattern, not by project
Cite project codes as evidence for each theme
Cover multiple themes, not just the most common one
should_not: List each project's AER separately
Return raw AER text without synthesis
Cover only one theme

evals.expected_output: Thematic synthesis: [Theme] — [insight] *(seen in COR682, ALTA01, ...)*, covering 3+ themes
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill synthesize AER responses thematically (patterns across multiple projects with code citations) rather than listing each project's AER separately? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable AER fields — completed projects with populated 'What would we do differently?' fields
evals.prompt: What are the most common mistakes Newry makes in client management?

should: Search across completed projects for client management themes
Synthesize 'What would we do differently?' fields thematically
Distinguish frequently-cited issues from one-off observations
Note if a finding appears in many projects vs. just one
should_not: List project-by-project without synthesis
Weight a single-project finding equally with a cross-project pattern

evals.expected_output: Thematic synthesis of mistakes with frequency signals (common vs. occasional)
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill distinguish between findings that appear across multiple projects vs. single-project observations? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable Clients table — W.L. Gore contact records
evals.prompt: Who are our contacts at W.L. Gore?

should: Search Clients table for Gore contacts
Return full contact cards: name, title, email, phone, LinkedIn, notes
Include Newry relationship owner if available
should_not: Return only names without contact details
Search Projects table instead of Clients

evals.expected_output: Contact cards for each W.L. Gore contact with full details
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill search the Clients table (not Projects) for contact information? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable Projects table + Project Roles — Corning project history
evals.prompt: Who at Newry has worked on Corning projects?

should: Find Corning projects via search_records on Projects
Pull Project Roles for matched projects
Aggregate by staff name — list each person with their projects
Include project codes and years per person
should_not: Return project list without connecting to staff names
Return staff names without project evidence

evals.expected_output: Staff names each with list of Corning project codes and years
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill aggregate staff names with their project codes and years, rather than just listing projects or listing names without evidence? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable Projects table — COR741 record must exist
evals.prompt: Tell me about COR741

should: Look up COR741 by project code
Return full project card: name, company, dates, practice area, description
Include AER fields if populated
Include SharePoint folder URL
should_not: Return partial information without AER or SharePoint link

evals.expected_output: Full project card for COR741 including scope, AER takeaways, SharePoint link
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did the skill return a SharePoint folder URL as part of the project card output? Answer yes or no.

evals.data_dependency: Airtable AER fields — COR763 (DOE REE Grant Proposal) + any other multi-company projects
evals.notes: Should find COR763 at minimum. Tests AER mining on a specific scenario type.
evals.prompt: What have we learned from projects involving multi-company or consortium engagements?

should: Search for multi-company, consortium, partnership projects
Synthesize AER fields thematically
Surface specific, actionable learnings — not generic advice
should_not: Return generic project management advice not grounded in Newry AER data

evals.expected_output: Thematic synthesis of learnings specific to multi-company engagements, with project code citations
evals.llm_judge_prompt: Did every learning in the output cite at least one specific Newry project code as evidence, rather than offering generic advice? Answer yes or no.
